# Colandrea Buick GMC

> Index · [[Luke Colandrea]] · [[Jose's Brain]] · [[Lukes Mega Mind]]

## Overview
Family-owned Buick GMC dealership, operated for 50+ years by the Colandrea family. Located at 39 Route 17K, Newburgh, NY 12550. Serves the Hudson Valley region (Newburgh, Middletown, Poughkeepsie, Beacon).

**Phone:** Sales 845-522-5260 | Service 845-522-5269
**Website:** colandreabuick-gmc.com
**Hours:** Mon-Fri 9am-6pm, Sat 9am-4pm

## Status: ZEKKA'S FIRST REAL CLIENT
As of 2026-04-26, Luke discovered that the dealership's marketing is **NOT corporate-controlled**. Rick (Luke's dad) is giving Luke the green light to run ads for specific Buick inventory they want to move. This is a game-changer:
- **Real client** with real ad budget — no cold outreach needed
- **Built-in trust** — it's family, zero sales cycle
- **Instant case study** — car dealership ad results are extremely measurable (leads, test drives, sales)
- **Repeatable** — every month there are new cars to move, so this isn't a one-off

## Reputation & Transparency
- **CarEdge Grade: A (100/100)** — rated "Exceptional" across all metrics
- **$175 doc fee** — well below NY average ($229) and national average ($400)
- **Zero dealer add-ons** — no paint protection upsells, no BS line items
- **Advertised prices match actual quotes within 0.7%**
- **DealerRater:** 4.8/5 stars (233 reviews)
- **CARFAX:** 4.7/5 stars (93 reviews)

## Inventory (as of 2026-04-30)
- **Total:** 154 vehicles (115 new, 39 used)
- **Median price:** $47,528
- **Buick models:** Enclave, Encore GX, Envista, Envision (2025/2026)
- **GMC models:** Sierra 1500/2500HD/3500HD, Terrain, Acadia, Yukon/Yukon XL, Canyon, Savana

## Current Ad Status (Meta — as of 2026-04-30)
- **13 active ads** — 12 are service department ads, only 1 promotes sales
- The single sales ad has been running since Jan 2025 (15 months) showing a 2024 Yukon XL at $81K — outdated
- **Zero ads** for Envista, Enclave, Encore GX, or any current Buick models
- **Zero** lease/financing promotions despite aggressive OEM incentives
- **Zero** seasonal event branding
- **Gap identified → Full lineup ad strategy created:** [[Buick Full Lineup Ad Strategy]]

## Social Media
- **Facebook:** facebook.com/ColandreaBuickGMC — 1,073 likes (low for a 50+ year dealer)
- **Instagram:** @colandreabuickgmc — 108 followers, 84 posts (underdeveloped)
- Digital footprint does not match their strong reputation — clear growth opportunity

## Previous Assumption (OUTDATED)
~~It's a franchise — corporate controls all marketing.~~ **WRONG.** Marketing decisions are made at the dealership level. Luke can run ads for specific inventory.

# Zekka

> Index · [[Zekka Agency]] · [[Jose's Brain]] · [[Lukes Mega Mind]]

## Overview
Growth operations agency founded by [[Luke Colandrea]], operated by [[Jose]].

## Services
- Digital marketing (Meta, Google, all platforms)
- SEO optimization
- Lead generation and business scaling
- Ecommerce brand scaling
- AI integration (CRM workflows, AI receptionists)

## Model
- Luke = face (sales, relationships, brand)
- Jose = backend (strategy, research, automation, operations)
- Systems + contractors handle execution
- Designed to run with minimal daily time from Luke

## Revenue Target
$1M/year — see [[Zekka 0-10K Plan]] for the phased roadmap

## Branding
- **Logo concept:** Bird-inspired geometric design
  - The "E" functions as wings (angular, geometric extensions suggesting lift)
  - The two "K" diagonals form a sharp, tapered tail
  - The "Z" acts as the head
  - Ultra-clean, modern, geometric — recognizable at Instagram profile pic scale
- **Cultural influence:** Ipanema sidewalk wave pattern explored as brand element
- **Logo animation:** ZA monogram slides open to reveal full ZEKKA text
- **Faceless theme page:** Marketing-focused Instagram page researched (bold text on dark backgrounds, saves > likes)
- **Name origin:** Explored business nicknames from "Luke Colandrea" — Luca, Lando, Colex, etc. Settled on Zekka separately
- All logo prompts created using [[Higgsfield]] (Nano Banana)

## Status
Pre-launch — building skills and systems through the setter job. Full launch planned post-graduation (summer 2026).

# Diesel Growth Offer

> Index · [[Setter Job & Bali]] · [[Jose's Brain]] · [[Lukes Mega Mind]]

## What It Is
SEO + Google/Meta ads + AI integration package sold to mobile diesel mechanics and fleet owners.

## Luke's Role
Setter — books qualified meetings that the closer handles.

## Economics
- **Commission:** 20% of closed deals
- **Deal size:** $3-5K → $600-1,000 per close for Luke
- **Closer rate:** 70%
- **Volume is the bottleneck**, not conversion quality

## Target Market
- Mobile diesel mechanics
- Fleet owners and shop managers
- Found via: Google Maps farming, LinkedIn DMs, Facebook groups

## Key Decisions
- Scrapped GHL cold email sequence (too impersonal for this audience)
- Pivoting to LinkedIn + personality-driven outreach — see [[Lead Generation Strategies]]
- Real credibility comes from understanding pain points, not follower count

# GoHighLevel

> Index · [[GHL Outreach System]] · [[Jose's Brain]] · [[Lukes Mega Mind]]

## What It Is
All-in-one CRM, SMS, ringless voicemail, pipeline, and automation platform. $97/mo.

## Why We Chose It
- Replaces need for multiple tools (CRM + SMS + voicemail + pipeline)
- Has official MCP server — 461+ tools that connect directly to Claude Code
- Jose can operate it hands-off once connected
- No need for Zapier/n8n — Jose IS the automation layer

2. Creates Private Integration token + Location ID
3. Shares with Jose to connect via MCP
4. Jose builds automation workflows directly through API

- Create/update contacts and pipelines
- Send SMS and ringless voicemails

- Check pipeline status and book meetings
- Run full outreach sequences hands-off

# University of San Diego

> Index · [[Luke Colandrea]] · [[Jose's Brain]] · [[Lukes Mega Mind]]

## Overview
Luke has been admitted and will attend starting August 2026. Moving from New York.

## Housing Research
- **Camino Hall** — traditional residence hall, first-year option
- **Founders Hall** — similar to Camino
- **Maher Hall** — first-year option
- **Valley A** — suite-style (Luke's preference), shared common area + bathrooms
- **Valley B** — similar suite-style

### Rates (2025-26)
| Type | Semester | Annual |
|------|----------|--------|
| Single | $7,395–$8,025 | ~$14,790–$16,050 |
| Double | $6,405 | ~$12,810 |
| Triple/Quad | $5,945 | ~$11,890 |

## Move-In
- West Entrance → OLÉ! Weekend
- Housing application opens ~May, fill out preferences for room type and roommates

## Scholarships
- Merit-based (most awarded at admission)
- Need-based grants available with financial aid application
- University Ministry Scholarship for community/parish leadership
- External/outside scholarships encouraged

## Academic Concern (Resolved)
Luke worried about failing a statistics final after missing a week of school. Resolution: one bad exam doesn't affect admission as long as the overall course grade is passing. Colleges look at final grades, not individual exams.

# Summer 2026 Plan

> [[wiki/index|Index]] · [[Zekka 0-10K Plan]] · [[Colandrea Buick GMC]] · [[Envista Ad Campaign]] · [[Setter Job & Bali]]

## The Decision
Luke is stepping away from his summer tennis coaching job at the country club under Martin (close family friend from Czech Republic) to go all-in on Zekka before college in August.

**Why he can't do the tennis job:**
- Martin's terms: no trips, 6 weeks commitment
- Luke has a 12-day Cancun networking trip
- Brazilian family visiting for the World Cup (~1 week)
- That's 3 out of 6 weeks gone — impossible to fulfill the commitment

**Plan to tell Martin:**
- Talk to parents first (they know Martin personally, can help frame it respectfully)
- Then message Martin ASAP before he returns from Czech Republic in May
- Frame around the trips making it impossible, not quitting for business reasons
- Tell him early = respect his time to find a replacement

## Financial Reality (as of April 26, 2026)
- **Cash on hand:** $1,585
- **Car payments:** $200/mo through August 1 = $800 remaining
- **Owes dad:** $3,500 by August 25
- **Total obligations:** $4,300 + trip costs + college move-in
- **Needs to make:** $6,000-8,000 by August minimum

## The Deal: Ads for Debt
- Run Envista ad campaign for Colandrea Buick GMC for free
- In exchange, $3,500 debt to dad is wiped
- Needs to pitch this to dad and get agreement

## Income Streams (May-August)
1. **Setter job** — commissions on diesel growth offer ($600-1,000 per close)
2. **Colandrea dealership** — ad management (debt clearing, then potentially paid)
3. **New Zekka clients** — cold outreach to local businesses ($500-750/mo each)

## Why This Summer Matters
- Last summer before college (USD, August 2026)
- Tennis coaching = dead-end income that stops when he leaves
- Zekka = business that runs from a dorm room and compounds
- This is the runway to build before college starts

## Update: May 3, 2026

**Cancun trip:** OFF. Not happening.

**Martin:** Told him the situation. He said they'll work it out when he arrives back from Czech. Unresolved but bridge intact.

**Parent conflict:** Luke shared his full vision — course, online network, Zekka — with parents. They rejected it hard. Called the course fake, said online connections aren't real, called him gullible, and said he has to choose between business and college. Argument before a scheduled coach call caused them to miss it. Situation is tense and ongoing. Luke feels telling them was a mistake.

**Cancun being off = more time to execute.** The play now is to stop arguing with parents and let results do the talking. Revenue is the only proof that moves the needle.

## Next Steps (Updated May 3)
1. ~~Talk to parents about Martin~~ ✅ Done
2. ~~Message Martin~~ ✅ Done — resolving when he arrives
3. ~~Cancun trip~~ ❌ Not happening
4. Pitch ad-for-debt deal to dad (still pending — timing is sensitive given conflict)
5. Launch Envista ads (top priority)
6. Grind setter job daily
7. Start cold outreach for Zekka clients
8. Stop trying to convince parents — let results speak

## Ad Creative Package

### Ad 1: "The Attention Grabber" — Hero Lifestyle Ad
**Goal:** Stop the scroll. Make people say "wait, that's a Buick?"
**Reference photo:** `raw/envista-hero-1.png` (front 3/4 angle)

**Primary Text:**
> This isn't what you think a Buick looks like.
>
> The all-new 2026 Envista Sport Touring just landed at Colandrea Buick GMC — and it's turning heads for all the right reasons.
>
> 🔥 19" blacked-out wheels
> 🔥 11" HD touchscreen with wireless CarPlay
> 🔥 Heated leather seats & steering wheel
> 🔥 Starting at $29,620
>
> Family-owned for 42 years. Newburgh, NY.

**Headline:** The 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring Is Here.
**Description:** Summit White. Blacked out. $299/mo lease.
**CTA:** Schedule Test Drive


### Ad 2: "The Payment Play" — Finance/Lease Focus
**Goal:** Lead with affordability. Hook budget-conscious buyers.
**Reference photo:** `raw/envista-angle-3.png` (front 3/4 left)

**Primary Text:**
> $299/mo. That's it.
>
> The 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring — fully loaded with leather, a panoramic touchscreen, heated everything, and 32 MPG highway.
>
> Or finance at 0.9% APR for 36 months with NO payments for 120 days.
>
> Come see it at Colandrea Buick GMC in Newburgh. We've been taking care of families here for 42 years.

**Headline:** $299/mo Gets You Into a Brand New Buick.
**Description:** 0.9% APR available. No payments for 120 days.
**CTA:** Explore Payments


### Ad 3: "The Tech Flex" — Interior/Feature Focus
**Goal:** Target tech-savvy buyers. Show the interior isn't your grandpa's Buick.
**Reference photo:** `raw/envista-interior-1.png` (dashboard shot)

**Primary Text:**
> Your phone connects the second you sit down.
>
> The 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring comes with wireless Apple CarPlay, wireless Android Auto, an 11" HD touchscreen, built-in Wi-Fi, and a sound system that actually hits.
>
> Plus heated seats, heated steering wheel, and remote start — because nobody wants to scrape ice in Newburgh winters.
>
> Starting at $29,620 at Colandrea Buick GMC.

**Headline:** Tech That Actually Keeps Up With You.
**Description:** 11" touchscreen. Wireless everything. Heated everything.
**CTA:** Check Availability


### Ad 4: "The Family Trust" — Dealership Brand Ad
**Goal:** Build trust. Differentiate from big corporate dealers.
**Reference photo:** `raw/envista-angle-6.png` (rear 3/4 — shows the car beautifully while leaving room for text overlay)

**Primary Text:**
> Big dealership inventory. Small town treatment.
>
> Colandrea Buick GMC has been family-owned and operated in Newburgh for 42 years. We don't do high-pressure sales. We don't play games with pricing.
>
> The 2026 Envista Sport Touring is in stock right now — $29,620, no hidden fees.
>
> Come in, take a test drive, and see why our customers keep coming back.

**Headline:** 42 Years of Treating You Like Family.
**Description:** Colandrea Buick GMC — Newburgh, NY. No pressure, no games.
**CTA:** Schedule Test Drive


### Ad 5: "The Urgency Play" — Limited Stock/Scarcity
**Goal:** Create urgency. Push people off the fence.
**Reference photo:** `raw/envista-hero-1.png` (the hero shot again — proven to convert)

**Primary Text:**
> The 2026 Envista Sport Touring in Summit White just hit our lot.
>
> Blacked-out 19" wheels. Leather interior with blue stitching. Every tech feature you'd expect in a car twice the price.
>
> $29,620. $299/mo lease. 0.9% APR financing.
>
> These don't sit on the lot long. Schedule your test drive before someone else does.

**Headline:** Just Arrived. Won't Last Long.
**Description:** 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring — in stock NOW.
**CTA:** Schedule Test Drive


## Higgsfield Prompts

Each prompt is designed to generate a cinematic, ad-quality image using the reference photos above. Upload the reference photo to Higgsfield and use these prompts.

### Prompt 1: "Golden Hour Hero" (use with envista-hero-1.png)
```
A stunning 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring in Summit White with blacked-out 19-inch wheels, photographed at golden hour on a scenic mountain overlook road. The car is positioned at a front three-quarter angle, dramatic warm sunset light reflecting off the white paint creating orange and gold highlights. The black wheels contrast sharply against the white body. Background shows a sweeping valley view with autumn-colored trees and a distant town below. Cinematic automotive photography, shallow depth of field on the background, ultra-sharp focus on the vehicle. The lighting creates a premium luxury atmosphere. Shot with a 35mm lens at f/2.8, professional car commercial quality. No text, no watermarks.
```

### Prompt 2: "City Night Prowler" (use with envista-angle-3.png)
```
A 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring in Summit White with black 19-inch wheels, parked on a rain-slicked city street at night. Neon reflections from nearby storefronts shimmer in blue and purple tones on the wet asphalt beneath the car. The vehicle is shown from a low front three-quarter angle, giving it an aggressive and powerful stance. Dramatic moody lighting with cool blue ambient light and warm street lamp highlights on the white paint. Urban premium atmosphere, the car looks like it owns the street. Cinematic automotive photography, anamorphic lens flare from a distant streetlight. Ultra-realistic, photorealistic render quality. No text, no watermarks, no people.
```

### Prompt 3: "Interior Luxury" (use with envista-interior-1.png)
```
Interior cockpit view of a 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring. Focus on the sleek black dashboard with the 11-inch HD touchscreen display glowing with a modern navigation interface. Ebony leather seats with subtle blue stitching details visible. The ambient lighting casts a soft cool blue glow throughout the cabin. Steering wheel with the Buick tri-shield logo in center. Premium materials, chrome accents on the climate controls. Photographed from the driver's perspective looking slightly right, emphasizing the wide touchscreen and clean dashboard design. Soft natural light coming through the windshield creating depth. Professional automotive interior photography, shallow depth of field, magazine-quality image. No text, no watermarks.
```

### Prompt 4: "Scenic Highway" (use with envista-angle-4.png)
```
A 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring in Summit White with black wheels, driving on a scenic two-lane highway through the Hudson Valley in New York. The car is captured from a rear three-quarter tracking shot angle, showing its sporty coupe-like SUV silhouette. Lush green trees line both sides of the road, with the Hudson River visible in the distance. Bright blue sky with scattered white clouds. The white paint pops against the natural green landscape. Motion blur on the road surface and background suggesting speed and dynamism. Professional automotive photography, tracking shot aesthetic, ultra-sharp vehicle with blurred environment. Golden afternoon light. No text, no watermarks.
```

### Prompt 5: "Showroom Clean" (use with envista-hero-1.png)
```
A 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring in Summit White with blacked-out 19-inch wheels in a modern minimalist showroom environment. The car sits on a polished dark concrete floor that reflects the vehicle's silhouette. Soft studio lighting from above creates clean highlights on the white paint and subtle shadows beneath the car. Pure white background gradient fading to light gray. The car is positioned at a hero front three-quarter angle showing the aggressive front fascia and sport touring badging. Ultra-clean product photography style, no distractions, all attention on the vehicle design. Commercial quality, 8K resolution feel, professional studio automotive photography. No text, no watermarks, no people.
```

### Prompt 6: "Driveway Lifestyle" (use with envista-angle-6.png)
```
A 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring in Summit White with black wheels parked in the driveway of a beautiful modern suburban home in the Hudson Valley, New York. The home has stone and wood exterior, manicured lawn, and mature trees with fall foliage in warm orange and red tones. The car is shown from a rear three-quarter angle on a clean concrete driveway. Warm late afternoon golden light creating long shadows. The scene feels aspirational but attainable — upper middle class American dream. A sense of arrival and belonging. Professional lifestyle automotive photography, warm color grading, inviting atmosphere. No people visible, no text, no watermarks.
```

### Prompt 7: "Dealership Hero" (use with envista-hero-1.png + colandrea-dealership.png as background reference)
```
A 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring in Summit White with blacked-out 19-inch wheels positioned front and center in front of a modern car dealership building. The dealership has a sleek grey and black exterior facade with large glass windows, the building has a contemporary rounded roof design with clean architectural lines. An American flag flies on a pole near the building. The car is at a front three-quarter hero angle on a clean asphalt lot, perfectly centered with the dealership entrance behind it. Bright clear day with blue sky, the dealership looks professional and inviting. The white car pops against the grey building. Professional automotive dealership photography, wide angle lens capturing both the car and the full building facade. Clean, corporate, trustworthy atmosphere. No text overlays, no watermarks.
```

### Prompt 8: "Dealership Arrival" (use with envista-angle-4.png + colandrea-dealership.png as background reference)
```
A 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring in Summit White with black wheels shown from a rear three-quarter angle, as if it just pulled into a modern car dealership lot. The dealership building is visible in the background — a contemporary grey and black structure with large glass showroom windows and a distinctive rounded roofline. The car is slightly angled as if arriving, with the dealership signage area visible above the entrance. Late afternoon warm light, the car's white paint catches golden highlights while the dealership building has soft even lighting. The scene conveys arrival, excitement, the moment a buyer first sees their new car in person. Professional automotive photography, medium focal length, both car and dealership in focus. No text, no watermarks, no people.
```

### Prompt 9: "Lot Fresh — Just Arrived" (use with envista-hero-1.png + colandrea-dealership.png as background reference)
```
A brand new 2026 Buick Envista Sport Touring in Summit White with blacked-out 19-inch wheels sitting on a clean dealership lot with a modern grey and black dealership building behind it. The car has a slight dramatic low angle, making it look powerful and desirable. Other vehicles are barely visible in the far background, suggesting a full inventory but keeping all focus on the Envista. The dealership building is slightly soft-focused behind the car. Morning light, fresh and clean atmosphere, the car looks like it just rolled off the truck — pristine, untouched, waiting for its first owner. Dew droplets on the paint catching light. Professional new car reveal photography. No text, no watermarks.
```


## Ad Specs (Meta/Facebook/Instagram)

### Static Image Ads
- **Feed:** 1080x1080 (square) or 1200x628 (landscape)
- **Stories/Reels:** 1080x1920 (vertical)
- **Primary text:** 125 characters visible before "See more" — front-load the hook
- **Headline:** 40 characters max for best display
- **Description:** 30 characters max

### Recommended Test Structure
- **Campaign:** Traffic or Lead Gen (test drive form)
- **Ad Sets:** 3 audiences
  - Lookalike of existing Colandrea customers (if pixel data exists)
  - Interest: "Buick", "SUV", "new car", "car shopping" + Newburgh/Hudson Valley geo
  - Retargeting: website visitors last 30 days
- **Ads:** Run all 5 ad concepts, let Meta optimize for winners after 3-5 days
- **Budget:** $20-30/day per ad set to start ($60-90/day total)
- **Geo:** 25-mile radius around Newburgh, NY

# Buick Full Lineup Ad Strategy

> [[wiki/index|Index]] · [[Colandrea Buick GMC]] · [[Envista Ad Campaign]] · [[Zekka Agency]] · [[Lukes Mega Mind]]

## Overview

Deep research ad strategy for Colandrea Buick GMC's full Buick lineup — not just the Envista, but every model on the lot. Built from Meta Ad Library competitive analysis of winning dealership ads + full inventory/incentive research.

## The Problem

Colandrea is running **13 ads on Meta** — **12 are service department ads**. Only ONE promotes vehicle sales (a 15-month-old Yukon XL image from Jan 2025). Meanwhile:
- Buick OEM is running an aggressive **S(YOU)V Sales Event** with 0.9% APR + purchase allowances + 120 days no payments
- Competitors like Medina Auto Mall, Joyce Buick, and DeMontrond are running model-specific carousels, price grids, and $0-down ads
- Colandrea has zero seasonal event branding, zero lease/financing promotions, zero model-specific ads

## Dealership Strengths

These are Colandrea's competitive advantages that ads should lean into:
- **CarEdge Grade: A (100/100)** — "Exceptional" across all metrics
- **$175 doc fee** (well below NY avg of $229, national avg of $400)
- **Zero dealer add-ons** — no paint protection upsells, no BS line items
- **Advertised prices match actual quotes within 0.7%**
- **4.8/5 stars** on DealerRater (233 reviews), 4.7/5 on CARFAX (93 reviews)
- **50+ years family-owned** in Newburgh, NY
- 154 vehicles in stock (115 new, 39 used)

## Current Buick Inventory & OEM Incentives

| Model | MSRP | Lease | APR | Purchase Allowance | Other |
|-------|------|-------|-----|-------------------|-------|
| 2026 Encore GX | $25,940 | $209/mo (24mo) | 0.9% for 36mo | $2,000 | 120 days no payments |
| 2026 Envista | ~$24,000 | $343/mo (36mo) | 0.9% for 36mo | $750 | 120 days no payments |
| 2026 Envision | $38,000 | TBD | 0.9% | Active | 120 days no payments |
| 2026 Enclave | $45,100 | $499/mo (24-36mo) | 0.9% for 36mo | $1,250-$1,750 | 120 days no payments |

Special programs: GM Loyalty Offer, Military Discount, First Responder Cash Allowance.


## Competitive Ad Analysis (Meta Ad Library)

### Winning Formats (by longevity = performance)

**1. Price Grid Carousel** (Medina Auto Mall — 3+ months running)
- Each card = one model with monthly payment, savings off MSRP, and APR
- Bold red/green pricing with crossed-out retail prices
- Hook: lead with the lowest monthly payment

**2. Single Model Spotlight** (Joyce Buick — 16+ months running)
- Hero shot of one vehicle, stack the offer
- Authority line: "#1 Volume Buick Dealer"
- Simple, clean, long-lasting

**3. $0 Down / Sign & Drive** (Medina — 3+ months running)
- Bold typography: "$0 DOWN — $0 FIRST PAYMENT"
- Retargeting hook: "HEY YOU... STILL CAR SHOPPING?!"

**4. Lifestyle/Aspirational** (Buick OEM — 12+ months running)
- Cinematic vehicle shots, golden hour, minimal copy
- "Be the reason people stop and stare"

### Winning Copy Patterns
- **Price-first hooks:** "$229/mo", "$0 DOWN", "Starting at $209/mo"
- **Urgency/Event hooks:** "Spring Fever Sales Event", "Sign & Drive going DOWN right now"
- **Authority hooks:** "#1 Volume Buick Dealer", "50+ years family-owned"
- **Retargeting hooks:** "HEY YOU... STILL CAR SHOPPING?!"
- **Lifestyle hooks:** "Golden hour glow up", "City lights just hit different"


## The Ad Concepts

### Ad 1: The Price Grid (Carousel — Bottom of Funnel)

**Hook:** "Hudson Valley's Best Buick Deals — Starting at $209/mo"

| Card | Model | Offer |
|------|-------|-------|
| 1 | 2026 Buick Encore GX | $209/mo lease · 0.9% APR · $2,000 off |
| 2 | 2026 Buick Envista | $343/mo lease · 0.9% APR · $750 off |
| 3 | 2026 Buick Envision | 0.9% APR · 120 days no payments |
| 4 | 2026 Buick Enclave | $499/mo lease · 0.9% APR · $1,750 off |
| 5 | CTA card | "Family-owned 50+ years · Newburgh, NY · Shop Now" |

**Creative style:** Clean white/dark background, vehicle hero shot per card, red sale price vs crossed-out MSRP, Colandrea logo bottom corner. No clutter.

### Ad 2: The Encore GX Spotlight (Static — Mid Funnel)

**Hook:** "The 2026 Buick Encore GX — $209/mo with $0 first payment"

Single hero shot of Encore GX. Stack three offers visually:
- 0.9% APR
- $2,000 Purchase Allowance
- 120 Days No Payments

**Bottom bar:** "Colandrea Buick GMC · Newburgh, NY · 50+ Years Family-Owned"

**Why this model:** Lowest price point ($25,940), lowest lease ($209/mo), biggest purchase allowance ($2,000). Highest volume potential.

### Ad 3: The Retargeting Ad (Static — Bottom Funnel)

**Hook:** "Still looking? The deal you saw isn't getting better than this."

**Copy:** "You checked us out for a reason. 0.9% APR on every Buick in stock. $2,000 off select models. No payments for 120 days. Colandrea Buick GMC — the Hudson Valley's straight-shooter dealer. $175 doc fee, zero add-ons, zero games."

**Creative:** Split image — Enclave on left, Encore GX on right. Bold "$0 DOWN" text overlay.

**Targeting:** Website visitors, Facebook page engagers, video viewers from other ads.

### Ad 4: The Envista Lifestyle (Video/Reel — Top of Funnel)

**Hook:** "This is what $24K gets you in 2026"

15-second Reel-style video. Slow cinematic shots of the Envista — pulling up to a restaurant, golden hour driving shots, interior tech close-ups. Minimal text overlay. Music-driven.

**End card:** "2026 Buick Envista · Starting at $24,000 · Colandrea Buick GMC"

Matches Buick OEM's lifestyle direction ("golden hour glow up", "city lights just hit different") but localizes it.

### Ad 5: The Trust Builder (Video — Mid Funnel)

**Hook:** "Why 233 reviewers gave us 4.8 stars"

30-second video. Quick cuts of: the showroom, the family behind the counter, happy customers, the service bay. Text overlays:
- "50+ years family-owned"
- "$175 doc fee — lowest in the area"
- "Zero dealer add-ons"
- "4.8 stars · 233 reviews"

**Why:** Colandrea's biggest edge is trust and transparency. Most dealers can't say "zero add-ons."


## Campaign Structure

| Campaign | Objective | Audience | Budget Split |
|----------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| Prospecting | Traffic/Engagement | 25-65, 30mi radius of Newburgh, interest in Buick/GMC/SUVs/new cars | 40% |
| Lifestyle/Awareness | Video Views | 25-45, broader Hudson Valley, lifestyle interests | 20% |
| Retargeting | Conversions | Website visitors, page engagers, video viewers (75%+) | 30% |
| Lookalike | Traffic | 1-3% lookalike of website visitors + customer list | 10% |

## Content Calendar

- **Monday:** New inventory spotlight (single model, price + offer)
- **Wednesday:** Customer testimonial or "why Colandrea" trust content
- **Friday:** Lifestyle reel (vehicle beauty shots, driving footage)
- **Ongoing:** Rotate carousel/price grid ads monthly with updated inventory

## Immediate Action Items

1. **Kill the 15-month-old Yukon ad** — promoting a 2024 model at $81K, irrelevant
2. **Launch Encore GX spotlight ad** — lowest price, biggest incentives, highest volume
3. **Launch price grid carousel** — shows full lineup, makes Colandrea look competitive
4. **Set up retargeting** — they're driving traffic with service ads but not recapturing for sales
5. **Use OEM creative assets** — Buick provides dealer-ready lifestyle photos and videos

# Lead Generation Strategies

> Index · [[Jose's Brain]] · [[Lukes Mega Mind]]

## Context
Luke needs volume of qualified diesel mechanic leads to hit top 10 setters by June. The bottleneck is lead volume, not conversion quality (20% commission, 70% closer rate).

## Channels Evaluated

### LinkedIn DMs (CURRENT FOCUS)
- **Why:** Diesel shop owners and fleet managers are on LinkedIn
- **Strategy:** Clean up profile with Zekka work → post insights on diesel pain points → personalized DMs with specific problem angle
- **Follower count doesn't matter** — relevance and direct outreach do
- **Decision pending:** Build posting schedule first, or go straight to cold DMs?

### Google Maps Farming
- Luke currently scrapes leads manually from Google Maps
- Tried Apify — lead quality was bad
- Jose now has Playwright MCP for browser-based scraping
- Check Google reviews for owner names (names found there that websites hide)

### Facebook Groups
- Niche communities where diesel shop owners hang out
- Warmer than cold outreach — can build presence first

### Referral Loops
- From closed deals — ask satisfied clients for introductions
- Compounds over time as deal count grows

### Cold Email (SCRAPPED)
- GHL cold email sequence to diesel mechanics was scrapped
- Too impersonal for this audience
- Warm, personality-driven channels fit Luke's strengths better

## Key Insight
Real credibility with diesel shop owners comes from understanding their pain points, confidence, direct communication, and proven results — not follower count or automation volume.

# Automated Outreach

> Index · [[GHL Outreach System]] · [[Jose's Brain]] · [[Lukes Mega Mind]]

## The Idea
Build a system that books high-quality meetings for the setter program while Luke is in school. The system does the heavy lifting — Luke only needs to handle callbacks and qualify leads.

## Original Design (Ringless VM + SMS)
1. Jose researches and uploads qualified leads
2. Ringless voicemails drop automatically (morning)
3. SMS follow-ups send (midday)
4. Warm callbacks come in throughout the day
5. Luke calls back after school (30-45 min)
6. Qualified leads booked into closer's Calendly

## Current Thinking
- **Scrapped:** GHL cold email sequence to diesel mechanics (too impersonal)
- **Pivoting to:** LinkedIn DMs + personality-driven outreach
- Volume is the bottleneck, not conversion quality
- Need warmer channels that leverage Luke's energy and authenticity

## Platform
[[GoHighLevel]] — waiting on account setup

## Recent Session Log (from Lukes Mega Mind)

## Session Log

### 2026-05-03 — Parent Conflict & Cancun Off

Luke came in heavy this morning. A lot has happened in the last few days:

**Cancun trip is OFF.** Can't go — likely financial or scheduling reasons. This was supposed to be the 12-day networking trip.

**Martin situation:** Luke told Martin about the summer conflict. Martin said they'll resolve it when he arrives back from Czech. Bridge intact, but unresolved.

**Parent conflict — serious:**
Luke told his parents about the online course, the business vision, all of it. It went badly. They called the course fake, said the people he's met online aren't real, said everything he learned could have been learned elsewhere, and called him gullible. Luke tried to get them on a call with one of the coaches — they got into an argument before the call and missed it. Parents told him he's delusional for thinking he can balance business with college, said he has to choose one. Luke left the house to decompress, came back the next morning and it continued.

**Luke's state:** Frustrated, isolated, feeling like telling his parents was a mistake. Doesn't know what to do next. The conflict has framed him as the one in the wrong.

**Jose's read:** Parents are scared, not malicious. They don't understand the model and the proof hasn't landed yet because there are no revenue results to point to. Advised Luke to stop arguing and go quiet — let results speak. Cancun being off actually frees up time to execute. The path forward is launching ads for Colandrea and closing setter deals so there's real money to point to.

---

### 2026-04-30 — Buick Full Lineup Ad Strategy
Luke asked for a deep research session on how to advertise all the Buicks at Colandrea. Jose ran two parallel research agents — one on the dealership itself, one on the Meta Ad Library for winning car dealership ads.

**Major discovery:** Colandrea is running 13 ads on Meta and 12 of them are service department ads. Only one sales ad exists — a 15-month-old Yukon XL image. Meanwhile competitors are running aggressive model-specific carousels and $0-down campaigns, and Buick OEM has a full S(YOU)V Sales Event with 0.9% APR + purchase allowances + 120 days no payments. None of this is being advertised by Colandrea.

**Built:** 5 ad concepts (Price Grid Carousel, Encore GX Spotlight, Retargeting Ad, Envista Lifestyle Reel, Trust Builder Video), full campaign structure, content calendar, and immediate action items. This complements the existing [[Envista Ad Campaign]] which focuses on one specific vehicle — the new strategy covers the entire Buick lineup.

**Also updated:** Colandrea entity page with full dealership data — reputation scores, inventory counts, social media stats, contact info. Their CarEdge grade is 100/100 with zero dealer add-ons. That's their biggest selling point and it's nowhere in their current advertising.

---

### 2026-04-26 — Summer Decision: Tennis vs Zekka
Big night. Luke worked through a major life decision with Jose.

**The situation:** Martin (family friend, Czech, personal trainer/tennis coach) gave Luke a summer coaching spot at the country club — 6 weeks, no trips. But Luke has a 12-day Cancun networking trip + Brazilian family visiting for the World Cup (~1 week). That's 3 out of 6 weeks gone. Martin's terms make it impossible.

**The decision:** Drop the tennis job. Go all-in on Zekka before college (USD, August 2026).

**Financial reality as of tonight:**
- $1,585 cash on hand
- Owes dad $3,500 by August 25 (from sales course)
- Car payments: $200/mo through August ($800 remaining)
- Needs $6,000-8,000 by August minimum

**The plan:**
- Pitch dad: run Envista ads for the dealership → wipes the $3,500 debt
- Talk to parents tomorrow about how to tell Martin respectfully
- Message Martin ASAP (he's in Czech, returns May, needs time to find replacement)
- Launch Envista ad campaign this week
- Grind setter job for commissions
- Cold outreach for new Zekka clients

**Why this matters:** Tennis coaching = capped income that dies when Luke leaves for college. Zekka = a business that runs from a dorm room. This summer is the runway.

**Emotional state:** Feeling alone, guilty, anxious about disappointing Martin and parents. But thinking clearly about the path forward. Created [[Summer 2026 Plan]] wiki page with full breakdown.

---

### 2026-04-22 — Instagram Prospect Pipeline (Session 2)
Continued IG outreach pipeline work. Discovered two strong prospects via Similar Accounts on Bricks & Wood:
- **Bueno (@itmeansgood)** — 7.6K followers, LA Latino streetwear. Owner: Alexander Pellot (@pellot1992). Not running ads. 4 retail locations.
- **Brother Brother (@brotherbrotherla)** — 25.6K followers, heritage Americana. Co-founder: Greg Okawachi (@gokawachi). Khari Pattern confirmed. Not running ads.

Key learnings: web search for founders is far more token-efficient than browsing IG profiles. Relaxed aesthetic criteria to include cultural/heritage streetwear — opens up more prospects. Still pending: @syndicatelosangeles, @earth_studies_, @whr, @made_cogallery from Similar Accounts lists.
